get.description:
The account is the root resource for many of the operations in the Bandwidth App.
The account is represented by an account ID, which is the resource that represents a Bandwidth customer, providing a root resource for all of the customer's attributes and services.
The API calls that are used to manage the details of a customer account, and to manage the resources that a Bandwidth customer has access to or control over, are accessed through the /accounts resource.
This API call retrieves information about the account indicated by the Account ID.

**Addresses**: `Address` and `LinkedAddresses`

- Address 
  - The service address of the account. Historically only US and Canadian addresses supported.
  - `Address` will continue to be populated as long as the address is US/ Canada based and only one linked address exists.
- LinkedAddresses 
  - A list of address reference IDs that are linked at the account. 
  - These addresses can be fully international.
  - `LinkedAddresses` will replace `Address`, however will continue to be supported for US/ Canada addresses.  
  - A list of **one** address will be supported initially, however this will extend as more address usages are introduced.
  - `LinkedAddresses` can be created and managed through the /addresses resource. See: [Addresses](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/numbers-apis/end-user-management/#tag/Addresses/operation/createAddress) for more information.
  - A link to the individual Address API is returned within this object, see `Link`.

post.description: Add a Sub-Account (Site) to the account

get.description: Retrieve a list of all Sub-Accounts (Sites) associated with the account
Retrieves the information associated with the Sub-Account (Site) ID

delete.description: Deletes the Sub-Account (Site). Sub-Accounts (Sites) can only be deleted if there are no SIP Peers attached to it

get.description: Retrieve information about a location (SIP Peer) or set of locations on sub-account (site)

post.description:
Create Location (SIP Peer).
Possible errors in response: https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/numbers/errors/#sippeervalidationerror

get.description: Retrieves the information associated with the location (SIP Peer) ID

put.description:
There are a few rules used to eliminate IP address collisions. The primary restriction is on the ability to share Term IP addresses across the Bandwidth API structural elements. Essentially...
  • Term Addresses cannot be shared anywhere
  • VoiceHost and VoiceHostGroup addresses can be shared between locations and can be different than or the same as Term IP Addresses
If the CallingName field is missing from the payload, CNAM will default to false on the location.
Possible errors in response: https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/numbers/errors/#sippeervalidationerror

delete.description:
Delete Location (SIP Peer).
Possible errors in response:
  • Delete not allowed while a Location (SIP Peer) has SMS feature

get.description: Retrieves the total number of seats on account
get.summary: Retrieve the total number of seats on account

get.description: Retrieves the total number of seats on a Location (SIP Peer)
Retrieve list of Associated Outbound Numbers associated with the Location. If none exist, an empty list is returned.

post.description: Submit a list of new Associated Outbound Numbers.
post.summary: Associate Phone Numbers with a Location

delete.description: Delete Associated Outbound Number from SIP Peer.

get.description: Retrieves the total number of seats on a Sub-Account (Site)
get.summary: Retrieve count of UC trunking seats by Sub-Account (Site)

phoneNumberCounts.name: phoneNumberCounts
phoneNumberCounts.description: Provides response payload with total phone number count for every Sub-Account on Account if specified as true

locationDetails.name: locationDetails
locationDetails.description:
Provides a response payload with a list of locations for every Sub-Account on Account with fields:
  • LocationId (PeerId)
  • LocationName (PeerName)
  • Description
  • IsDefaultLocation (IsDefaultPeer)
  • Address
  • FinalDestinationUri
  • PremiseTrunks
  • CallingName
  • SsSendToCustomer

Also each Sub-Account will contain a count of locations on it:

  • SipPeerCount

summary.name: summary
summary.description: Provides response payload with list of basic information about every Location for every Sub-Account on Account and overrides sipPeerDetails parameter if specified as true

paginationPageQueryParam.description: Retrievals must be paginated, using page and size indicators.  The page parameter contains the page number, e.g. "1" in the case of the first page
paginationPageQueryParam.name: page

paginationSizeQueryParam.description: The size parameter indicates the number of Associated Outbound Numbers to return in the payload
paginationSizeQueryParam.name: size

Location.description: The URL of the created Location (SIP Peer)

createLocationWithVoiceHostsXmlExample.description: Create a location with voice hosts.
createLocationWithVoiceHostsXmlExample.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<SipPeer>
   <PeerName>name</PeerName>
   <Description>description</Description>
   <IsDefaultPeer>false</IsDefaultPeer>
   <FinalDestinationUri>uri</FinalDestinationUri>
   <VoiceHosts>
      <Host>
         <HostName>"10.10.10.1"</HostName>
      </Host>
      <Host>
         <HostName>10.10.10.2</HostName>
      </Host>
   </VoiceHosts>
   <TerminationHosts>
      <TerminationHost>
         <HostName>2.1.1.9</HostName>
         <Port>0</Port>
         <CustomerTrafficAllowed>DOMESTIC</CustomerTrafficAllowed>
      </TerminationHost>
      <TerminationHost>
         <HostName>2.1.1.96/30</HostName>
         <Port>0</Port>
         <CustomerTrafficAllowed>DOMESTIC</CustomerTrafficAllowed>
      </TerminationHost>
   </TerminationHosts>
   <Address>
      <HouseNumber>1600</HouseNumber>
      <HouseSuffix />
      <PreDirectional />
      <StreetName>PENNSYLVANIA AVE NW</StreetName>
      <StreetSuffix />
      <PostDirectional />
      <AddressLine2 />
      <City>WASHINGTON</City>
      <StateCode>DC</StateCode>
      <Zip>20500</Zip>
      <PlusFour />
      <County />
      <Country>United States</Country>
      <AddressType>Service</AddressType>
   </Address>
   <PremiseTrunks>PremiseTrunks</PremiseTrunks>
   <CallingName>
      <Display>true</Display>
      <Enforced>true</Enforced>
   </CallingName>
   <SsSendToCustomer>NONE</SsSendToCustomer>
</SipPeer>

ucTrunkingConfiguration.description: For UC Trunking accounts the UcTrunkingConfiguration element describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The Type parameter is one of Seats, Premise, or Cloud, and the UsageCategory parameter is one of UC250, UC500 or UC1000.

For UC Trunking accounts the UcTrunkingConfiguration element describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The Type parameter is one of Seats, Premise, or Cloud, and the UsageCategory parameter is one of UC250, UC500 or UC1000.

voiceHosts.description: These addresses, comprised of HostName and optional Port, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls to for Origination services. The VoiceHosts list of IP addresses used for an active/standby address selection mechanism, where the first address is attempted, followed by the second address and so on. Except under failure situations the first address in the list is preferred. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or Fully Qualified Domain Name

voiceHostGroups.description: The VoiceHostGroups element is comprised of one VoiceHostGroup element, which is used to randomly distribute traffic amongst up to 10 IP addresses. Failover behavior is retained within the group

terminationHosts.description: These addresses, comprised of IP or Subnet(CIDR format) and optional Port, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls to for Termination services. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or subnets. In case of subnet you should specify NetworkAddress of subnet as IP

originationRoutePlanJson.description: The OriginationRoutePlan element consists of an action and a list of prioritized and weighted routes that tell Bandwidth where to send phone calls to phone numbers associated with the route plan.

voiceHosts.description: These addresses, comprised of a `hostName` and an optional `port`, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls for origination services. The voiceHosts list of IP addresses are used for an active/standby address selection mechanism, where the first address is attempted, followed by the second address, and so on. Except under failure situations, the first address in the list is preferred. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or a fully qualified domain name.

voiceHostGroups.description: The VoiceHostGroups element is comprised of one VoiceHostGroup element, which is used to randomly distribute traffic amongst up to 10 IP addresses. Failover behavior is retained within the group

terminationHosts.description: These addresses, comprised of IP or Subnet(CIDR format) and optional Port, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls to for Termination services. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or subnets. In case of subnet you should specify NetworkAddress of subnet as IP

addressInconsistencies.description: Indicates discrepancies found in the provided address details

UcTrunkingConfiguration.description: For UC Trunking accounts the UcTrunkingConfiguration element describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The Type parameter is one of Seats, Premise, or Cloud, and the UsageCategory parameter is one of UC250, UC500 or UC1000.

For UC Trunking accounts the UcTrunkingConfiguration element describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The Type parameter is one of Seats, Premise, or Cloud, and the UsageCategory parameter is one of UC250, UC500 or UC1000.

VoiceHostGroups.description: The VoiceHostGroups element is comprised of one VoiceHostGroup element, which is used to randomly distribute traffic amongst up to 10 IP addresses. Failover behavior is retained within the group

hosts.description: These addresses, comprised of HostName and optional Port, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls to for Origination services. The VoiceHosts list of IP addresses used for an active/standby address selection mechanism, where the first address is attempted, followed by the second address and so on. Except under failure situations the first address in the list is preferred. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or Fully Qualified Domain Name

originationRoutePlan.description: The OriginationRoutePlan element consists of an action and a list of prioritized and weighted routes that tell Bandwidth where to send phone calls to phone numbers associated with the route plan.

locationTerminationHosts.description: These addresses, comprised of IP or Subnet(CIDR format) and optional Port, are used by the Bandwidth network to send calls to for Termination services. Maximum of 10 hosts - can be IP address or subnets. In case of subnet you should specify NetworkAddress of subnet as IP

VoiceHostGroups.description: The VoiceHostGroups element is comprised of one VoiceHostGroup element, which is used to randomly distribute traffic amongst up to 10 IP addresses. Failover behavior is retained within the group

billingCycle.description: BillingCycle is the day of the month that the account's billing cycle closes. Valid values are 1, 5, 15, and 20.

customerSegment.description: CustomerSegment is used to classify accounts for reporting and business purposes.

default911Provider.description: The Default911Provider is the 911 provider that will be used for emergency services if no other 911 provider is specified on a phone number or order.

externalId.description: An external identifier that can be used to associate the Bandwidth account with an account in another system.

globalAccountNumber.description: Global Account Number (GAN), A globally unique identifier for the account. This value is often used when integrating with Bandwidth systems.

maxTnsForAutomatedPort.description: The maximum number of telephone numbers that can be included in an automated port order for this account. If not specified, the system default will be used. (Values greater than '49' are invalid for Manual TN Overflow thresholds.)

nenaId.description: NENA ID is the National Emergency Number Association (NENA) identifier for the account. This value is used for E911 services.

penaltyType.description: The PenaltyType indicates whether or not a penalty will be applied to port-in orders for this account.

portCarrierType.description: The PortCarrierType indicates the type of carrier for porting purposes.

pricing.description: Pricing is used to specify the pricing model for the account.

spid.description: SPID is the Service Provider Identifier for the account. This value is used for E911 services.

addressLine2.description: AddressLine2 is used to specify Unit, Suite, Floor, etc. in the Service Address. AddressLine2 is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

city.description: City is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

country.description: Country is the country of the ServiceAddress. This value will be derived from the StateCode, so it should generally be omitted. If provided, it must be the name of the country or country code, for example 'United States' or 'US'.

county.description: The county where the subscriber or business is located.

houseNumber.description: The HouseNumber is the street address number of the ServiceAddress. HouseNumber is mandatory for port-in orders in which the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

housePrefix.description: The HousePrefix is the non-numeric address number prefix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

houseSuffix.description: The HouseSuffix is the non-numeric address number suffix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

plusFour.description: PlusFour is the 4 digits that are sometimes suffixed to the Zip Code.

postDirectional.description: The PostDirectional is the street name post directional of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

preDirectional.description: The PreDirectional is the non-numeric street name prefix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

stateCode.description: StateCode is the 2-letter abbreviation of the state of the ServiceAddress. StateCode is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

streetName.description: The StreetName is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

streetSuffix.description: The StreetSuffix is the street suffix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

zip.description: Zip is the Zip Code of the ServiceAddress. Zip is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

isDefaultLocation.description: Value is true or false. The default location (SIP peer) is the default "destination" for any phone numbers that are ordered for the subaccount (site) in which the location resides.  Each subaccount can have only 1 default location. You can configure multiple locations on a subaccount.

hostName.description: The IP address or host name of the address to be used for the voiceHosts or voiceHostGroups addresses

port.description: Optional port number for voice and termination hosts. This is an optional parameter with default values that are dependent on the application.

originationRoutePayloadName.description: A friendly name for a route.  This is not used for any back-end call routing.

originationRoutePayloadEndpoint.description: Must be one of the following: - a NANP 10-digit telephone number - a SIP URI - an IPv4 address - a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN)

customerTrafficAllowed.description: A TerminationHost can be configured to allow different customer traffic types.  Allowed values are LITE, DOMESTIC and ALL. This is an optional parameter

customerProvidedId.description: Customer can provide an optional id. Note that the customer can use the same id across multiple orders.

customName.description: Customer can provide an optional name

ucType.description: For UC Trunking accounts the Type parameter describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The Type parameter is one of Seats, Premise, or Cloud.

ucUsageCategory.description: For UC Trunking accounts the UsageCategory parameter describes the kind of UC trunking that is provided. The UsageCategory parameter is one of UC250, UC500 or UC1000.

linkRel.description: the relationship of the link to the resource

linkMethod.description: the HTTP method to be used with the link

data.description: the main data payload of the response

classDescription.description: Description of the A2P Message Class

associatedOutboundNumberType.description:
An Associated Outbound Number should:

  • Be a valid phone number in E.164 format. For example: +18005550123.
  • Not be duplicated. An Associated Outbound Number is unique within an account.

associatedOutboundNumberErrorReason.description:
Describes the reason an associated outbound number has failed. This could be due to validation or business logic.
Possible Error Reasons include:

  • Invalid number format.
  • Number is already associated with location ${location_name} [ID: ${location_id}].
  • Associated Outbound Number not found with location [ID: ${location_id}].

supportLevel.description: Support tier level for the account

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic ZGVtbZpwQDU1dzByZA==`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to protected resources.
- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.
For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management)
